executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Tool enforcement relies solely on prompt text; model routinely skips critical tool calls and hallucinates successful execution.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Add code-level validation gate that blocks the response when required tool calls are absent.

evidence_pack.summary: Model responded with tool result text without issuing a tool_call. No tool execution recorded for that turn.
Session context carried stale tool result from turn 3 into turn 12 without re-validation.
Tool requirement expressed only in system prompt text. No code-level validation that required tools were called.

findings.title: Operational answer can bypass real tool validation
findings.symptom: Model generates a response that looks like a tool result but was never validated by the actual tool execution pipeline.
findings.user_impact: Users receive confident but incorrect operational guidance (e.g., access granted when it should be denied).
findings.mechanism: The tool router checks for tool_call presence in the model output, but does not verify the tool was actually executed. Model hallucinates tool usage by embedding result-like text.
findings.root_cause: Tool enforcement is prompt-only; no code gate validates tool execution before forwarding the answer.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a code-level validator that blocks responses unless the required tool_call was actually executed and returned a valid result.

findings.title: Same-session stale evidence reuse without freshness check
findings.symptom: A tool result from turn 3 is reused as live evidence in turn 12 without re-probing the tool.
findings.user_impact: Decisions are made against outdated data, producing wrong conclusions with high confidence.
findings.mechanism: The distillation pipeline injects a compressed summary of earlier turns, which includes the turn-3 tool result. No freshness guard prevents reuse beyond its TTL.
findings.root_cause: Distilled artifacts re-enter the same session without a time-to-live or staleness marker.
findings.recommended_fix: Attach a freshness timestamp to each distilled artifact. Reject artifacts older than the tool's documented TTL during answer shaping.

conflict_map.note: Turn-3 tool result is carried forward without a staleness check, causing turn-12 to act on expired data.
The same tool result is referenced in both the raw session history and the distilled summary, consuming tokens twice.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Enforce tool freshness probes at code level
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Without a code gate, the model will continue to hallucinate valid tool usage, producing confident-wrong answers.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Eliminates the critical-path for confident-wrong operational behavior.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Reduce distilled artifact re-entry into same session
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Stale evidence reuse corrupts correctness even when the model is otherwise sound.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Prevents same-session pollution; reduces false-positive confidence.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Migrate internal flow to typed JSON envelopes
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Freeform markdown in distillation makes it impossible to attach structured staleness metadata.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Enables automatic freshness validation and reduces context token waste.
